questions.question: What are the four essential components required to formulate any problem in AI? Provide a brief explanation of each.
expectedPoints: Initial state - the starting point of the problem
Goal state - the desired outcome or condition
Set of actions/operators - legal moves that transform states
Path cost function - measures the cost of paths through the state space

questions.question: Explain the concept of problem space with examples. How does the nature of the problem space influence the choice of search algorithm?
expectedPoints: Problem space represents all possible states reachable from initial state
Can be represented as graphs (states as nodes, actions as edges)
Examples: 8-puzzle, water jug problem, route finding
Solution depth determines required search depth
Observability affects whether complete or partial information algorithms are needed
Deterministic vs. stochastic nature influences algorithm choice
